mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) after a prolonged period of job losses signals a definitive trough in the business cycle. This confirmation of recovery boosts investor confidence in future corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: Monthly change in PAYEMS > 0 following at least 6 consecutive months of negative changes.
evidence_refs: fact:As of July 2009, PAYEMS has been contracting for 18 consecutive months during the GFC.
failure_condition: In the 12 months after the trigger condition is met, the cumulative 60-day S&P 500 return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which constitutes a majority of economic activity. This strength translates directly into higher corporate revenues and justifies higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the largest component of GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero-lower bound, an acceleration in CPI inflation is bullish for equities. It signals escaping a deflationary trap, indicates rising aggregate demand, and lowers real interest rates, thereby boosting asset valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has held the FEDFUNDS rate near zero since December 2008.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the correlation between the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward returns is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a contraction in the economy's productive capacity. This signals weakening corporate activity and lower future earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has experienced a severe contraction during the 2008-2009 recession.
failure_condition: When the monthly change in INDPRO is negative, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high unemployment rate (UNRATE) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates stagflationary pressure. This erodes real incomes and corporate profit margins, creating a toxic environment for equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% and 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:The unemployment rate is currently 9.4% and has been rising sharply.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in housing starts (HOUST) from a deeply depressed level is a powerful leading indicator of economic recovery, given the sector's large multiplier effects on employment and consumption. This signals renewed confidence and drives equity prices higher.
candidate_trigger: 3-month percentage change in HOUST > 20% after HOUST is below 50% of its prior 5-year peak.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market was the epicenter of the GFC, with HOUST collapsing by over 75% from its peak.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A large positive spread between the year-over-year growth rate of real GDP (GDPC1) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that monetary policy is highly accommodative relative to the real economy's trajectory. This excess liquidity supports risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:Policy rates are near zero while real GDP growth, though currently negative, is expected to rebound sharply.
failure_condition: The correlation between the spread (YoY % change in GDPC1 - FEDFUNDS) and 60-day forward returns is not positive and significant over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpectedly large single-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) acts as a negative shock to investor sentiment, triggering immediate risk-off behavior and negative short-term equity returns as future growth expectations are revised downwards.
candidate_trigger: Monthly change in UNRATE > +0.3 percentage points.
evidence_refs: fact:Monthly labor reports are high-impact market events.
failure_condition: When the monthly change in UNRATE exceeds +0.3pp, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative.
